category.name: Fonts
category.preferred_workflow: Use Aspose.Words font APIs with correct type usage and explicit namespace disambiguation.

strict_api_bans: Do not use using System.Drawing in this category.
Do not use bare Color or bare Font when ambiguity is possible.
Do not assume source documents, folders, or external font files already exist.
Do not invent unsupported font APIs, embedding APIs, or substitution hooks.
canonical_patterns: Use Aspose.Words.Font for document text formatting through Run, Style, Paragraph, or DocumentBuilder objects.
Use Aspose.Drawing.Font only for true drawing-font scenarios, with an explicit fully qualified type name.
Use fully qualified Aspose.Drawing.Color and explicit System.Drawing.Color only where the target API requires disambiguation.
Bootstrap local sample documents and validate that requested outputs are written.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Compile errors because Font is ambiguous between Aspose.Drawing.Font and Aspose.Words.Font.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use fully qualified names and use Aspose.Words.Font for document formatting versus Aspose.Drawing.Font only for real drawing scenarios.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Compile or assignment issues because Color is ambiguous or the wrong color type is used for the target property.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use fully qualified Aspose.Drawing.Color or explicit System.Drawing.Color conversion only where the Aspose.Words API expects it.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: A drawing font object is treated as if it were a document text-formatting object.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Apply document font values through Run.Font, Style.Font, ParagraphBreakFont, or DocumentBuilder.Font.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Examples assume existing documents, folders, or font resources already exist.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Create local sample documents and deterministic local scenarios inside the example.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Examples save output but do not verify that the intended font change, report, or render actually occurred.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Validate the changed font property, configured font setting, or output file existence before finishing.

examples.task: Retrieve the Font object from a Paragraph's first Run using Paragraph.Runs[0].Font.
Use DocumentBuilder.Font.Name to set default font for newly inserted content before adding text.
Use DocumentBuilder to set default font size for all subsequently inserted paragraphs.
Define a style that sets font name, size, and color, then apply it to selected text.
Apply a predefined style that includes specific font settings to a range of paragraphs.
Set the font name of a Run to Arial via the Font.Name property.
Change the font size of a Run to fourteen points using the Font.Size property.
Assign a blue color to a Run's font by setting Font.Color to System.Drawing.Color.Blue.
Apply bold and italic styles to a Run by setting Font.Bold and Font.Italic to true.
Apply underline style to a Run by setting Font.Underline to UnderlineType.Single.
Apply bold, italic, and underline simultaneously to a Run using respective Font properties.
Set text fill color to red and thirty percent transparency using Font.Fill.Color and Font.Fill.Transparency.
Apply a semi‑transparent fill to text by setting Font.Fill.Color and Font.Fill.Transparency together.
Set the underline type to double wave for a Run by assigning Font.Underline to UnderlineType.DoubleWave.
Adjust a paragraph's line spacing to 1.5 lines by assigning Font.LineSpacing to 1.5.
Set line spacing to double for a paragraph by assigning Font.LineSpacing to 2.0.
Programmatically reset line spacing to default for all paragraphs by setting Font.LineSpacing to 0.
Apply East Asian emphasis mark Dot to a Run by setting Font.EmphasisMark to EmphasisMark.Dot.
Apply East Asian emphasis mark only when document language is Japanese by checking language before setting Font.EmphasisMark.
Retrieve and display the EmphasisMark value of a Run for debugging East Asian formatting.
Retrieve and display the current font size of a Run for debugging purposes.
Define a custom font substitution rule that replaces missing Times New Roman with Calibri.
Configure font substitution to map missing Garamond to a locally installed Georgia font.
Enable automatic font substitution for missing fonts during document loading by configuring FontSettings.
Create a custom font substitution table and load it from an XML configuration file.
Embed all used fonts into the document when saving by enabling the EmbedFullFonts option.
Enable font embedding when saving documents to PDF to ensure visual fidelity across platforms.
Reset all font attributes of a Run to defaults by calling Font.ClearFormatting.
Copy font formatting from one Run to another using Run.Font.Clone method.
Create a reusable method that applies a specific font name and size to any given Run.
Create a Font object, set its size and color, then assign it to multiple Runs for consistency.
Programmatically replace all occurrences of a specific font with another across the entire document.
Adjust font color dynamically based on paragraph index using a loop and conditional logic.
Implement batch processing to set Helvetica font for all Runs in a collection of documents.
Load a document, change all headings to use a bold sixteen‑point font, then save.
